gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
ism: noun. An ideology, system of thought, or practice that can be described by a word ending in -ism.
miming: verb. present participle and gerund of mime
minim: noun. A half note, drawn as a semibreve with a stem.
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.
nims: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of nim
sign: noun. A visible fact that shows that something exists or may happen.
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
sing: verb. To produce musical or harmonious sounds with one’s voice.
swim: verb. To move through the water, without touching the bottom; to propel oneself in water by natural means.
swimming: noun. The act or art of sustaining and propelling the body in water.
swing: verb. To rotate about an off-centre fixed point.
wig: noun. A head of real or synthetic hair worn on the head to disguise baldness, for cultural or religious reasons, for fashion, or by actors
wing: noun. An appendage of an animal 's (bird, bat, insect) body that enables it to fly.
wising: verb. present participle and gerund of wise
gim: noun. Edible seaweed in Korean cuisine.
imi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anamgura.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
mings: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ming
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
simi: noun. A short sword or dagger used by the Kikuyu people.
snig: verb. To drag a log along the ground by means of a chain fastened at one end.
wim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wik-Mungkan.
wimmin: noun. Eye dialect spelling of women.
